title: (Senior) Medical Director - Nephrology, EMEA (Client - dedicated)
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


At Thermo Fisher Scientific, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life \- enabling our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while taking science a step beyond through research, development and delivery of life\-changing therapies. With clinical trials conducted in 100\+ countries and ongoing development of novel frameworks for clinical research through our PPD clinical research portfolio, our work spans laboratory, digital and decentralized clinical trial services. Your determination to deliver quality and accuracy will improve health outcomes that people and communities depend on – now and in the future.

**Discover Impactful Work:**


The (Senior) Medical Director will provide medical support and medical advice for clinical trials and safety evaluation within our client environment.

**A day in the Life:**

* ### **Provide medical leadership to the study teams, including creating clinical trial protocols while implementing the latest scientific information, contribute to site selection, involved in review and approval of study protocols and amendments, Clinical Study Reports (CSRs), Informed Consent Forms (ICFs), Case Report Forms (CRFs), protocol deviation decisions and other clinical documents as required for the conduct of clinical trials**
* ### **Real time Medical Monitoring of clinical studies: ensure medical validity of each individual primary endpoint as well study subject safety, while ensuring medical issues are identified early. Pro\-active customer concern and correction of issues in consultation with the Global Medical Indication Lead**
* ### **During initial review of protocol, consults with internal groups and support staff regarding procedural and budgetary items and the necessity for change due to any subsequent protocol amendments. Acquires other functions’ dedication to close gaps and address issues timely and effectively. Serves as chief liaison between sponsor and all PPD internal departments performing the required tasks during all study phases. Educates Investigator sites by delivering a protocol specific lab procedures presentation and demonstration during an investigator meeting. Controls, handles and follows day to day activities during the course of the clinical trial to resolve any issues and answer queries**
* ### **Medical point of contact for all internal and external stakeholders: Interact with the investigators as needed, answer questions of IRBs and Health Authorities, prepare and present material to the study executive and independent safety committee if applicable; medical training of site staff at Investigator meetings; CRA training in new indications**
* ### **Ensure study compliance for all medical aspects by understanding and applying all relevant SOPs and GCP**
* ### **Closely collaborate with the cross functional study team members in order for medical risks, issues, and results to be clear to all involved parties**

**Education**

* Medical Doctor, with a specialty in Nephrology, shown clinical experience running nephrology\` patients in hospital practice
* Industry experience in clinical development required

**Knowledge, Skills, Abilities**

* Significant experience within pharmaceutical industry, clinical trials and pharmaceutical medicine is critically important
* Fluent in spoken and written English
* Shown understanding and experience with NDA submission process.
* Shown understanding of regulatory guidelines for adverse event reporting
* Strong communication \& presentation skills and is a strong teammate


Our Mission is to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. Watch as our colleagues explain 5 reasons to work with us. As one team of 100,000\+ colleagues, we share a common set of values \- Integrity, Intensity, Innovation and Involvement \- working together to accelerate research, solve complex scientific challenges, drive technological innovation and support patients in need. \#StartYourStory with PPD, part of Thermo Fisher Scientific, where diverse experiences, backgrounds and perspectives are valued.

**Apply today! http://jobs.thermofisher.com**


Thermo Fisher Scientific is an EEO/Affirmative Action Employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, disability or any other legally protected status.

title: Medical Science Liaison Rhinology and Gastroenterology
description:
**Qualifica professionale**: Medical Science Liaison Rhinology and Gastroenterology

* *Area di competenza: Centro Italia*

  



**Informazioni sulla posizione**

Sanofi Specialty Care è la Business Unit di Sanofi, focalizzata su Immunologia, Oncologia, Malattie Rare e Sclerosi Multipla. Il nostro scopo è quello di aiutare le persone con patologie debilitanti e complesse, spesso difficili da diagnosticare e trattare. Ci dedichiamo alla scoperta e al Progresso di nuove terapie, fornendo nuove speranze ai pazienti e alle loro famiglie in tutto il mondo.


La posizione, inserita nella Direzione Medica Immunologia e a diretto riporto dell’MSL Lead Rinologhy e Gastroenterology, rappresenta l’estensione della Direzione Medica sul territorio, ed è responsabile di stabilire, sviluppare e mantenere relazioni scientifiche “win\-win” tra pari (“peer\-to\-peer”) durature e di alto livello con i KOL (Key Opinion Leader) della propria area geografica di competenza. Contribuisce, in allineamento alla strategia medica della propria area terapeutica, a creare partnership costruttive e inter\-funzionali con stakeholder interni ed esterni, che siano in grado di garantire uno *Scientific Exchange* non promozionale, così come lo sviluppo di sempre maggiori opportunità di business in relazione al portfolio prodotti della propria area terapeutica.

**Responsabilità principali:**

* Operare nel rispetto della strategia di prodotto e dell’area terapeutica (Esofagite Eosinofila e Rinosinusite cronica con Poliposi Nasale) in collaborazione con il Medical Team di sede e le altre funzioni aziendali;
* Contribuire ad assicurare l'accesso al mercato per i prodotti in lancio relativamente all’area terapeutica e/o geografica di propria competenza;
* Sviluppare e mantenere un network stabile con i Key Opinion Leaders (ad es. Otorino, Allergologi, Gastroenterologi) attraverso la condivisione delle informazioni scientifiche e la raccolta di input provenienti dalla comunità scientifica. Pianificare, eseguire e tracciare il KOL engagement plan della macro area di pertinenza;
* Essere responsabile della diffusione e scambio con i KOL di informazioni scientifiche accurate, nuove e rilevanti per i prodotti della propria area terapeutica, come lo stato d’avanzamento della patologia, i bisogni clinici non “soddisfatti” (unmet medical need), così come le nuove indicazioni terapeutiche e le nuove pratiche nella cura dei pazienti tramite incontri organizzati e condotti singolarmente (face to face) o in gruppo (medical meeting);
* Partecipare a incontri e congressi scientifici nazionali e internazionali con lo scopo di raccogliere opinioni ed insight strategici da parte dei principali stakeholder all’interno del proprio target medico;
* Raccogliere proposte di studio da parte dei medici specialisti sul territorio ed effettuare una prima valutazione di attinenza con la strategia di prodotto e dell’area terapeutica;
* Promuovere la generazione e implementazione del local medical plan (networking multidisciplinari infra e tra strutture, supporto a data generation ed eventi educazionali, momenti di confronto one\-to\-one e one\-to\-group, sia in presenza che da remoto).

***Profilo del/la candidato/a***

* **Esperienza**: pregressa esperienza all'interno della Direzione Medica,
* **Competenze tecniche e “soft skill”**: Capacità di lavorare in team e di lavorare in team multifunzionali; Ottime capacità di comunicazione tra pari e maturata esperienza nella realizzazione di presentazioni scientifiche; Flessibilità e adattamento, proattività e condivisione di materiali fra le varie funzioni aziendali coinvolte; Abilità a lavorare in modo autonomo ed indipendente e capacità di self study, pianificazione, definizione delle priorità, problem solving, analisi di business; Autorevolezza e capacità di persuasione; Capacità di costruire solide relazioni interpersonali e relazionali.
* **Istruzione**: Laurea conseguita in discipline scientifiche (Medicina e Chirurgia/ Biologia/ Biotecnologie/ CTF o Farmacia);
* **Lingue**: Ottima conoscenza della lingua italiana ed inglese (parlata e scritta).
* **Altro**: Costituisce requisito imprescindibile la residenza in Toscana o Emilia\-Romagna, preferibilmente a Firenze o Bologna.

***Perché scegliere noi?***

* Dai vita ai miracoli della scienza insieme a un team solidale e orientato al futuro.
* Scopri infinite opportunità per far crescere il tuo talento e dare impulso alla tua carriera, sia che si tratti di una promozione o di un semplice trasferimento, sul territorio nazionale o all'estero.
* Usufruisci di un pacchetto di incentivi ponderato e ben costruito che riconosce il tuo contributo e amplifica il tuo impatto.
* Prenditi cura di te e della tua famiglia, grazie a un'ampia gamma di benefit per la salute e il benessere, tra cui assistenza sanitaria di alta qualità, programmi di prevenzione e benessere e almeno 14 settimane di congedo parentale senza distinzione di genere.

**Perseguire il** *progresso*. **Scoprire lo** *straordinario*.


Il progresso non avviene senza le persone: persone con background diversi, in luoghi diversi, con ruoli diversi, ma tutte unite da una cosa sola: il desiderio di far accadere miracoli. Lei può essere una di queste persone. Inseguendo il cambiamento, abbracciando nuove idee ed esplorando tutte le opportunità che abbiamo da offrire. Perseguiamo il progresso. E scopriamo insieme lo straordinario.


In Sanofi offriamo pari opportunità a tutti, a prescindere da origine etnica, colore, ascendenza, religione, genere, origine nazionale, orientamento sessuale, età, cittadinanza, stato civile, disabilità o identità di genere.


Guarda il video ”One day at Sanofi” e dai un’occhiata alle nostre iniziative nell’ambito di Diversità, Equità e Inclusione sul sito sanofi.com


\#LI\-EUR


\#LI\-Onsite

title: Medical Science Liaison Rare Neurology (Lombardia, Veneto, Trentin- Alto Adige, Friuli-Venezia Giulia, Emilia-Romagna)
description:
**Location** Milan, Lombardy, Italy **Job ID** R\-254857 **Date posted** 29/06/2026
  
Are you ready to elevate scientific engagement in rare neurology and help shape standards of care for patients across Northern Italy? Do you thrive at the intersection of rigorous science, cross‑functional collaboration, and field leadership that turns insights into better outcomes?


As our **Medical Science Liaison in Rare Neurology,** you will be the trusted scientific partner across Lombardia, Veneto, Trentino‑Alto Adige, Friuli‑Venezia Giulia, and Emilia Romagna. You will build and deepen relationships with leading clinicians and researchers, accelerate the flow of high‑quality medical information, and translate real‑world insights into actions that advance patient care. Your work will directly influence research, evidence generation, and scientific exchange that matters in clinics and communities. You will join a rare disease medical team with the energy of a biotech and the reach of a global organization.


In this role, you will set a high bar for excellence in execution, provide mentorship across the field team, and contribute to strategies and programs that improve outcomes for underserved patient populations.


**Accountabilities:**


**KOL Engagement and Scientific Exchange:** Build trusted, long‑term relationships with regional KOLs; deliver timely, balanced, and compliant medical information; and drive high‑quality medical‑to‑medical interactions that deepen disease and treatment understanding.


**Evidence Generation and Research Support**: Plan and support research initiatives for assigned products; partner with Italian centers involved in national and international clinical development; and collaborate closely with Clinical Operations to ensure quality, compliance, and impact.


**Local Study Excellence:** Support the Medical Advisor on local studies by shaping protocols, enabling accurate data management and analysis, preparing final reports, and ensuring timely, ethical dissemination of results.


**Insight to Strategy:** Systematically capture medical insights from field interactions and translate them into clear, data‑driven recommendations that inform strategy and elevate scientific impact in partnership with the Medical Advisor.


**Regulatory and Commercial Partnership:** Provide high‑quality support to Regulatory Affairs on registration dossiers and related documents; partner with Marketing to uphold scientific and educational standards through material review and the planning of scientific events.


**Congress and Medical Programs Leadership:** Lead the medical presence at national and European congresses; staff medical booths; facilitate advisory boards; and guide engagement with local scientific societies to strengthen peer‑to‑peer exchange.


**Data, Digital, and AI Enablement:** Define outcome metrics and KPIs; present data‑driven results and recommendations to leadership; design or recommend digital implementations (e.g., Veeva, Salesforce, Tableau) and AI use cases for Medical Affairs; contribute to SOP updates and best‑practice playbooks.


**Regional Ownership and Compliance:** Manage all activities and account relationships in line with Company Values, Code of Conduct, internal procedures, and Italian legislation; ensure meticulous governance in every interaction across the region.


**Leadership and Mentorship:** Mentor new hires and less‑experienced MSLs through onboarding, coaching, and ongoing development; oversee specific processes and projects across the MSL team to support the Therapeutic Area Lead and drive excellence in execution.


**Autonomy and Accountability:** Operate independently with limited guidance; take full accountability for results; and consistently raise the bar on operational performance and scientific impact across Northern Italy.


**Essential Skills/Experience:**


* Proven Medical Affairs/MSL experience: 2\+ year in Medical Affairs/MSL roles.
* Strategic insight generation: demonstrated ability to synthesize insights from KOL engagements, advisory boards, congresses, and medical inquiries into actionable, compliant recommendations that improve patient care.
* Strong leadership and interpersonal skills, including feedback delivery, rolemodeling best practices, and fostering a collaborative learning culture.
* Independent working style and accountability: ability to operate with limited oversight, set and track outcome metrics/KPIs, and present datadriven results and recommendations to TAL/leadership.
* Digital and data proficiency: familiarity with Veeva, Salesforce; capability to lead AI/datadriven pilots and contribute to change management.
* Crossfunctional collaboration: effective partnership with Medical Advisors and broader teams (e.g., Clinical, Market Access, Regulatory, Compliance, Marketing).
* Compliance and ethical standards: strong knowledge of applicable regulations and legislation relevant to pharmaceutical industry.
* Scientific communication: excellent written and verbal communication in English and Italian; ability to facilitate complex medical discussions and deliver clear presentations to diverse audiences.
* Project and research support skills: experience in protocol planning, data management and analysis, reporting, and timely publication processes.
* Advanced scientific degree (MD, PharmD, PhD, or equivalent) in Neurology, Neuroscience, or related biomedical discipline.

**Desirable Skills/Experience:**


* Field experience in rare neurology; direct exposure in clinical research, evidence generation, or medical education.
* Established network with neurologists and multidisciplinary centers across Lombardia, Veneto, Trentino‑Alto Adige, Friuli‑Venezia Giulia, and Emilia Romagna.
* Track record leading congress activities, advisory boards, and medical programs at national or European level.
* Experience with real‑world evidence generation, registries, and outcomes research in rare disease.
* Advanced analytics capability with CRM/analytics tools and dashboards (e.g., Veeva reporting, Salesforce analytics, Tableau) and familiarity with medical‑compliant AI applications.
* Strong project leadership in cross‑functional settings including Regulatory, Market Access, and Compliance during pre‑ and post‑launch phases.
* Additional European language proficiency beyond Italian and English.

**Why AstraZeneca:** Join a rare disease team where bold science and swift execution meet real patient stories. You will work in an environment that blends the autonomy and pace of a biotech with the resources and reach of a global organization, bringing unexpected teams together in the same room to unlock new ideas and accelerate impact. We pair ambition with care—valuing kindness alongside high standards—so you can stretch your capabilities with tailored development while staying close to the lived experiences that guide our work. Here, your expertise turns into action that shapes evidence, informs decisions, and brings therapies to people who need them most.


**Call to Action**: Ready to lead scientific engagement in rare neurology and help define standards of care across Northern Italy? Step forward and shape what comes next with us.


**We offer:**


Salary compensation: 73\.048,00 € \- 109\.572,00 €


Contract Level: A3


CBA: Chemical\-Pharmaceutical


Full\-time


Permanent position


**Benefits:**


In addition to the Gross Base Salary, we offer:


* An annual target bonus, governed by the Company’s policies as amended from time to time and subject to periodic review
* Wellbeing benefits (welfare, insurance, psychological support, etc.)
* Company Car

Final compensation will be determined based on a variety of factors, including but not limited to skills, experience, internal organizational equity and other relevant factor.

30\-giu\-2026
**Closing Date**


14\-lug\-2026

title: Junior MSL Gyn/GU Lombardia - Piemonte (sostituzione maternità)
description:
**Location** Milan, Lombardy, Italy **Job ID** R\-255256 **Date posted** 29/06/2026
  
Sei pronto a mettere la tua competenza scientifica al servizio di medici e pazienti per cambiare l’esito della cura nei tumori ginecologici e genito\-urinari? In questo ruolo collegherai dati di frontiera alle decisioni cliniche quotidiane, accelerando l’adozione responsabile di terapie che possono trasformare la pratica clinica.


Ti vedi come punto di riferimento credibile per specialisti e istituzioni, capace di portare dal campo insight che orientano strategie e lanci? Lavorerai a stretto contatto con colleghi commerciali e di accesso, contribuendo con evidenze e formazione mirata a elevare la qualità delle interazioni scientifiche e l’efficacia delle iniziative sul territorio. Entrerai in un contesto che unisce rigore scientifico e mentalità imprenditoriale: qui impariamo in fretta, proviamo nuove strade e misuriamo l’impatto, con l’obiettivo concreto di migliorare l’esperienza di cura per le persone che vivono con il cancro.


**Responsabilità:**


**Leadership scientifica sul territorio:** Agire da esperto di riferimento per l’area terapeutica GYN/GU, fornendo approfondimenti cruciali e sostenendo un coinvolgimento medico di alta qualità con i professionisti sanitari.


**Formazione mirata e continua**: Definire, erogare e perfezionare programmi di formazione personalizzati per team interni (vendite/commerciale) e per operatori esterni (operatori sanitari, istituzioni), garantendo un trasferimento accurato delle conoscenze e un utilizzo ottimale dei prodotti.


**Standard e conformità:** Implementare e far rispettare standard, politiche e procedure per le attività mediche sul campo, promuovendo l’efficienza e il rispetto delle linee guida interne e di settore.


**Relazioni e credibilità**: Sviluppare e coltivare solide relazioni interne ed esterne per facilitare lo scambio scientifico e mantenere la credibilità con gli stakeholder del settore sanitario.


**Ricerca e insight azionabili:** Avviare, supportare o guidare progetti di ricerca specializzati e report interni, traducendo l’evidenza in spunti concreti a supporto delle funzioni commerciali.


**Sorveglianza scientifica e normativa:** Mantenersi aggiornati su letteratura scientifica, tendenze del settore, normative e best practice per garantire che le attività sul campo siano adeguate e a norma.


**Cultura di apprendimento:** Promuovere una cultura di apprendimento continuo, sviluppo delle competenze e conformità all’interno del team di Affari Medici.


**Impatto iniziale:** Nei primi mesi, mappare i centri chiave GYN/GU, stabilire relazioni prioritarie e lanciare i primi moduli formativi, portando insight immediati alle funzioni cross\-funzionali.


**Impatto a medio termine:** Contribuire ai lanci e al miglioramento continuo della qualità delle interazioni scientifiche, scalando programmi educativi con indicatori chiari di valore clinico e di business.


**Competenze/Esperienza essenziali:**


* Laurea triennale o titolo equivalente con formazione professionale continua;
* Conoscenza approfondita del settore farmaceutico, idealmente con una solida esperienza in ruoli di Medical Science Liaison;
* Spiccate capacità di gestione delle relazioni e abilità di lavorare efficacemente con stakeholder interni ed esterni;
* Conoscenza delle vendite, del marketing, della compliance e della legislazione farmaceutica in tutti i mercati.
* Domicilio nell’area di competenza

**Competenze/Esperienza preferenziali:**


* Laurea specialistica (MD, PhD, Pharm D o equivalente)
* Comprovata esperienza nella gestione di progetti o team in ambito scientifico o di affari medici;
* Comprovata capacità di erogare programmi di formazione medica.

**Perché AstraZeneca:** Qui la scienza incontra l’intraprendenza: lavorerai in un team dedicato all’oncologia che valorizza curiosità, coraggio e crescita, con accesso privilegiato a ricerche di punta, tecnologie avanzate e un pipeline ricco di lanci in più Paesi. Mettiamo attorno allo stesso tavolo profili diversi per sbloccare idee audaci, prendiamo decisioni rapide e sosteniamo rischi calcolati, sempre guidati dall’evidenza e dall’attenzione ai pazienti. La tua voce conta: potrai co\-creare strategie, vedere direttamente l’impatto del tuo lavoro e sviluppare competenze di leadership, in un ambiente che valorizza la gentilezza tanto quanto l’ambizione.


**Invito all’azione**: Se vuoi trasformare scienza e insight in benefici reali per le persone con tumori GYN/GU e far crescere rapidamente la tua carriera, invia la tua candidatura oggi e inizia a lasciare il segno!


**La nostra offerta**


Fascia salariale: 38\.678,40 € \- 58\.017,60 €


Livello contrattuale: C2


CCNL: Chimico\-farmaceutico


Full\-time


Sostituzione Maternità


**Benefits:**


Oltre alla Retribuzione Annua Lorda, prevediamo anche:


* Bonus annuale secondo le policy aziendali tempo per tempo vigenti e soggetto a revisione periodica
* Premio di partecipazione secondo le policy aziendali tempo per tempo vigenti e soggetto a revisione periodica
* Wellbeing benefits (welfare, assicurazioni, supporto psicologico etc) secondo le policy aziendali tempo per tempo vigenti e soggetto a revisione periodica
* Auto aziendale;

La retribuzione finale sarà determinata in base a diversi fattori, tra cui, a titolo esemplificativo ma non esaustivo, competenze, esperienza, equità interna all’organizzazione ed altri fattori rilevanti.

title: Associate Sales Engineer Director
description:
**Join us on our exciting journey!**


IQVIA™ is The Human Data Science Company™, focused on using data and science to help healthcare clients find better solutions for their patients. Formed through the merger of IMS Health and Quintiles, IQVIA offers a broad range of solutions that harness advances in healthcare information, Technology, analytics and human ingenuity to drive healthcare forward.


**The job:**

Manages pre\-sales technical activities for the development and implementation of complex products/applications/solutions.
  

  

**Essential Functions**
  

• Provides technical knowledge, advice and support to sales representatives, resellers and customers during the pre\-sales process to resolve requests regarding product/service technical issues and maximize the benefits derived from the organization's products and/or services.
  

• Assists customers during the sales process to maximize the benefits derived from the organization's products and/or services.
  

• Manages pre\-sales technical activities for the development and implementation of complex products/applications/solutions.
  

• Provides technical input into bid proposals, projects and technical documents with the sales process, and may identify additional sales opportunities with existing customers.
  

• Develops detailed customer installation and/or system integration plans.
  

• Oversees pre\-sale technical support in sales presentations and product demonstrations.
  

• May publish technical articles regarding specific applications.
  

• Works with marketing and product managers to define new products.
  

• May serve as company liaison on specific technical projects with customers.
  

• May provide product feasibility analysis in support of sales efforts with sales representatives.
  

• May select, develop, and evaluate personnel to ensure efficient operation of the function.
  

• Can operate across like minded technology applications.
  

• Follows consistent processes.
  

• Shares and owns best practices.
  

  

**Qualifications \- our ideal candidate will have**
  

• Bachelor's Degree with 5 years of related experience Req Or
  

• Master's Degree with 5 years of experience Req Or
  

• Ph.D. with 3 years of experience Req
  

• **5 years of field sales engineering experience Req**


* **Fluency in English mandatory**


**PLEASE SUBMIT YOUR CV IN ENGLISH \- THIS ROLE IS NOT OPENTO VISA SPONSORSHIP!**  

  

We know that meaningful results require not only the right approach but also **the right people**. Regardless of your role, we invite you to reimagine healthcare with us. You will have the opportunity to play an important part in helping our clients drive healthcare forward and


**Whatever your career goals, we are here to ensure you get there!**


**We invite you to join IQVIA™.**


IQVIA is a leading global provider of clinical research services, commercial insights and healthcare intelligence to the life sciences and healthcare industries. We create intelligent connections to accelerate the development and commercialization of innovative medical treatments to help improve patient outcomes and population health worldwide. Learn more at https://jobs.iqvia.com


IQVIA is committed to integrity in our hiring process and maintains a zero tolerance policy for candidate fraud. All information and credentials submitted in your application must be truthful and complete. Any false statements, misrepresentations, or material omissions during the recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of your application, or termination of employment if discovered later, in accordance with applicable law. We appreciate your honesty and professionalism.


The potential base pay range for this role, when annualized, is €63,700\.00 \- €108,500\.00\. The actual base pay offered may vary based on a number of factors including job\-related qualifications such as knowledge, skills, education, and experience; location; and/or schedule (full or part\-time). Dependent on the position offered, incentive plans, bonuses, and/or other forms of compensation may be offered, in addition to a range of health and welfare and/or other benefits.

Prin Value, Access & Policy Specialist
description:
Careers that change lives start here. Medtronic is a global leader in healthcare technology with a Mission to alleviate pain, restore health, and extend life. Our 95,000 employees work across more than 150 countries to put patients first — developing innovative medical technologies that improve the lives of 72\+ million patients each year. Your unique talents will help shape the future of healthcare while building a career grounded in purpose, growth, and impact.


**A Day in the Life**
=====================

You are committed to improving patient access to innovation and developing solutions that make a real difference. You thrive in a dynamic and cross\-functional environment, where the ability to influence stakeholders and lead complex projects can directly impact patient outcomes. If you are driven by purpose and motivated to shape healthcare systems, this is an opportunity to make a meaningful impact at Medtronic.
  

Join Medtronic Italy’s Value, Access \& Policy team, where your work will accelerate access to Medtronic’s life\-changing therapies. You will support the development and execution of Market Access strategies for breakthrough technologies across multiple Operating Units. This role is critical in shaping how therapies are funded, adopted and integrated into the healthcare system, working closely with national and regional stakeholders to overcome access barriers and enhance care delivery.
**Responsibilities may include the following and other duties may be assigned:**

* Support the development and execution of market access and reimbursement strategies to enable patient access to Medtronic therapies within the Italian healthcare system
* Identify key access barriers and contribute to the design and implementation of innovative solutions, including outcome\-based models and local pilot initiatives
* Collaborate cross\-functionally with Sales, Marketing, Market Development, and Government Affairs to ensure alignment between access strategies and business priorities
* Partner with EMEA HEPR teams to support local data generation and the development of robust value propositions for Medtronic therapies
* Engage with regional healthcare authorities and key stakeholders to support the co\-creation of sustainable access and funding solutions
* Provide market access and reimbursement insights to internal stakeholders and contribute to the development of training materials
Support the planning, execution, and monitoring of key Market Access projects, ensuring effective coordination and tracking of outcomes  
* 

**Required Knowledge and Experience:**

title: Regulatory Affairs Specialist (medical device / cosmetici)
description:
**Olcelli Farmaceutici** è una storica e consolidata realtà italiana specializzata nello sviluppo e nella produzione di specialità farmaceutiche, dispositivi medici e cosmetici. Coniughiamo tradizione industriale e innovazione costante, ponendo la massima attenzione alla qualità e alla sicurezza. Per il potenziamento del nostro team, cerchiamo un/a Regulatory Affairs Specialist.

Inserito/a nel Team Quality \& Regulatory, la risorsa gestirà la conformità normativa dei prodotti aziendali. In particolare si occuperà di:

* **Dispositivi Medici:** Gestione strategica e operativa della **transizione normativa** (adeguamento ai requisiti del Regolamento MDR 2017/745\), redazione/aggiornamento dei Technical File e della documentazione di sorveglianza post\-market (PMS/PMCF). Gestione delle registrazioni su EUDAMED e Ministero della Salute.
* **Cosmetici:** Validazione delle formule, revisione del packaging/etichettatura (claim) e redazione/mantenimento del PIF (Product Information File). Gestione delle notifiche sul portale CPNP.
* **Interfaccia Enti Esterni:** Relazione con Organismi Notificati, laboratori e autorità regolatorie
* **Audit e Qualifica Fornitori:** Pianificazione e conduzione di **audit ispettivi presso i fornitori** (di materie prime, packaging e servizi critici) per garantirne la qualifica e il mantenimento degli standard qualitativi richiesti.
* **Conformità Chimica (REACH e CLP):** Verifica della conformità delle materie prime e delle sostanze chimiche in conformità ai regolamenti REACH e CLP. Supervisione e aggiornamento delle Schede di Dati di Sicurezza (SDS) e corretta classificazione/etichettatura dei prodotti.

title: Cluster V&A Director
description:
**Cluster V\&A Director**
at Kyowa Kirin International plc
Milan \- Italy **WE PUSH THE BOUNDARIES OF MEDICINE.**  

**LEAPING FORWARD TO MAKE PEOPLE SMILE**  

At Kyowa Kirin International (KKI), our purpose is to make people smile. This means more than drug discovery and development; it is about embedding care into everything we do to make a difference every day for those that need it most. We’re an inclusive pharmaceutical company that takes time to understand what really matters to our patients, their families, and their healthcare professionals, helping our people to take bold actions that deliver life\-changing solutions sooner. Our culture is rooted in our values: Teamwork, Commitment to Life, Innovation, and Integrity. They help us to push boundaries to deliver extraordinary impact and make KKI a brilliant place to work. **Job Purpose**  

As Cluster Value \& Access Director – Southern Cluster, you will be at the forefront of improving patient access to Kyowa Kirin’s innovative medicines. In this highly visible leadership role, you will develop and execute market access, pricing, and payer engagement strategies that support both current and future products.
Partnering with local, regional, and global teams, you will translate insights into impactful value propositions, lead HTA and reimbursement activities, and shape access strategies that deliver sustainable value for patients, healthcare systems, and Kyowa Kirin. This is an exciting opportunity to influence key access decisions and drive commercial success across the Southern Cluster. **Key Responsibilities:*** Shape and deliver pricing, reimbursement, and market access strategies across the Southern Cluster (Italy, Spain, Portugal), driving optimal patient access and commercial success.
* Develop compelling value propositions, HTA submissions, value dossiers, and HEOR materials to support reimbursement and pricing objectives.
* Partner with affiliate, regional, and global teams to anticipate market access challenges and translate insights into actionable strategies.
* Lead payer engagement initiatives and innovative access solutions that maximize product value and reimbursement opportunities.
* Monitor healthcare policies, market dynamics, and pricing trends to identify opportunities and risks across the portfolio.
* Collaborate cross\-functionally with Regulatory, Medical, Clinical, and Commercial teams to ensure evidence generation meets payer needs.
* Serve as the subject matter expert for Value \& Access and Health Economics \& Outcomes Research (HEOR), providing strategic guidance across the organization.
* Support pipeline planning by assessing future market access requirements and influencing global development strategies.
* Lead and contribute to strategic market access projects while promoting Kyowa Kirin’s values and reputation internally and externally.

 **Position Requirements**  

* Advanced degree in Health Sciences, Economics, Pharmacy, Medicine, or a related field (PhD and/or postgraduate qualifications in Market Access, HEOR, or Business preferred).
* Significant experience in pharmaceutical Market Access, Pricing \& Reimbursement, Government Affairs, or HEOR, with a strong track record of successful access outcomes.
* Deep understanding of reimbursement systems, health economics, pricing strategies, and payer decision\-making.
* Proven ability to influence senior stakeholders and drive cross\-functional collaboration in complex environments.
* Strong commercial acumen, strategic thinking, and project leadership skills.
* Experience developing HEOR evidence and supporting market access submissions.
* Fluent in English and the local cluster language; additional languages are an advantage.
* Existing network within healthcare authorities and payer organizations is highly desirable.

### **Key Competencies**


✔ Strategic and commercially minded  

✔ Strong leadership, communication, and negotiation skills  

✔ Highly analytical with excellent problem\-solving abilities  

✔ Influential relationship builder across internal and external stakeholders  

✔ Self\-motivated, proactive, and results\-driven  

✔ Innovative thinker with a patient\-focused mindset  

✔ Comfortable working independently and in cross\-functional teams  

✔ Strong project and budget management capabilities  

✔ Embodies Kyowa Kirin's values of **Commitment to Life, Teamwork, Innovation, and Integrity**.

description:
**We Make Life More Rewarding and Dignified**


**Location**: Milan
  

**Department**: Sales

  

Attualmente siamo alla ricerca di un ***Patient Service Advisor***, da inserire nell’ufficio Patient Service.


**RUOLO E RESPONSABILITA’**


In quanto *Patient Service Advisor*, sarete responsabili di fornire un servizio di qualità ed eccellenza a coloro i quali usano i nostri prodotti e servizi, accompagnandoli lungo tutto il loro percorso di vita, garantendo loro soluzioni di valore.
Vi occuperete di avviare, sviluppare e mantenere la relazione con gli utenti finali, interagendo attivamente tramite supporto telefonico, invio di prodotti in prova e pronta risoluzione delle loro problematiche, grazie alle conoscenze tecnico pratiche dei *Medical Device* e all’interazione con i clienti esterni (Ambulatori, Ospedali, Farmacie etc.) ed interni (personale *field*, *Customer Service* etc.).
Parteciperete all'implementazione di attività di marketing, comunicazioni, eventi e iniziative con l'obiettivo di acquisire e fidelizzare nuovi clienti, offrendo al contempo una *customer experience* di livello superiore.
Siete naturalmente predisposti alle relazioni interpersonali e desiderate contribuire con le vostre competenze ed energie al miglioramento della qualità di vita dei nostri utilizzatori e, nel farlo, al raggiungimento dei risultati aziendali
Svolgendo la vostra attività quotidiana, sarete guidati dalla cultura e dai principi immutabili di Hollister: integrità, servizio, dignità della persona e *stewardship*.  

**Core Activities*** Utilizzo dei software aziendali, in particolare CRM
* Gestione delle chiamate in entrata e in uscita attraverso tutti i canali di comunicazione (principalmente per telefono ed e\-mail, in misura minore per eventi in presenza)
* Mantenimento e sviluppo delle relazioni con i clienti per consigliare e promuovere i nostri prodotti, servizi e il loro utilizzo
* Invio dei campioni e degli strumenti educativi ai clienti, in base ai loro bisogni
* Gestione di follow\-up proattivo e regolare dei contatti: raccolta e aggiornamento delle informazioni, col fine di arricchire il database.
* Collaborazione con la forza vendita per ottimizzare l'assistenza ai clienti
* Record e tracking dei reclami sui prodotti

  

**HARD SKILLS**


* Ottime capacità espositive
* Inserimento rapido di dati
* Rispetto delle procedure di elaborazione delle informazioni
* Ottima conoscenza dei principali sistemi informatici (internet, outlook, power point, excel)
* Conoscenza di SFDC apprezzata
* Utilizzo delle basi delle tecniche di vendita telefonica (negoziazione, relazioni con i clienti) e personalizzazione della propria proposta di vendita in base al profilo e alle reazioni del cliente
* Conoscenza della lingua inglese per semplificare i rapporti con l’Headquarter e la partecipazione a progetti internazionali

  

**Soft SKILLS**


* Ottima capacità di ascolto (interessarsi sinceramente agli altri, ascoltare con attenzione e fare le domande giuste)
* Doti empatiche indispensabili
* Eccellenza nel servizio (mettere il cliente al centro delle nostre preoccupazioni, capire le sue esigenze, le sue modalità operative e le sue abitudini di consumo, in modo da anticipare le sue richieste e servirlo nel miglior modo possibile)
* Gestione dello stress
* Ottime capacità relazionali
* Flessibilità
* Winning mindset (apertura a nuove sfide, entusiasmo, adattamento al cambiamento, capacità di prendere l’iniziativa)

**Cosa offriamo**


* contratto iniziale di 12 mesi finalizzatoi ad assunzione
* Lo stipendio base previsto per questa posizione è circa 28\.000\-35\.000 all'anno. Lo stipendio effettivo varia in base alla posizione, all'istruzione, all'esperienza, alle competenze, alle capacità e ad altri fattori aziendali legittimi del candidato
* 30 giorni di ferie annuali
* Assicurazione infortuni
* Ticket restaurants
* Possibilità di smart working e flessibilità
* Opportunità di formazione e crescita
* Employee Assistance Program (EAP)

**About Hollister Incorporated**
  

Hollister Incorporated is an independent, employee\-owned company that develops, manufactures and markets healthcare products worldwide. The company spearheads the advancement of innovative products for ostomy care, continence care and critical care, and also creates educational support materials for patients and healthcare professionals. Headquartered in Libertyville, Illinois, Hollister has manufacturing and distribution centers on three continents and sells in nearly 80 countries. Hollister is a wholly owned subsidiary of The Firm of John Dickinson Schneider, Inc., and is guided both by its Mission to make life more rewarding and dignified for people who use our products and services, as well as its Vision to grow and prosper as an independent, employee\-owned company, and in the process, to become better human beings.


**EOE Statement**
  

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or protected veteran status.

title: Sales Specialist
description:
UpSurgeOn is an innovative MedTech company based in Milan, dedicated to transforming surgical education through hyper\-realistic physical simulators combined with augmented and virtual reality technologies. Its neurosurgical training solutions are used by hospitals, universities, and medical societies across the globe, supporting the next generation of surgeons in developing hands\-on skills before entering the operating room.

  

  

**Posizione:**
--------------

The Sales Specialist will drive revenue growth by identifying, engaging, and converting prospective customers — including hospitals, universities, medical societies, and distributors — into long\-term UpSurgeOn partners. The role combines proactive outbound sales activity with relationship management of existing accounts, contributing directly to the company's international commercial expansion.

**Responsibilities**

* Identify and develop new business opportunities within assigned territories or market segments (hospitals, universities, medical training centers, distributors).
* Manage the full sales cycle: prospecting, qualification, product demonstration, negotiation, and closing.
* Build and maintain strong relationships with Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs), clinical decision\-makers, and procurement stakeholders.
* Deliver compelling product presentations and demonstrations of UpSurgeOn's simulation and AR/VR training solutions to clinical and academic audiences.
* Achieve and exceed individual sales targets and KPIs as defined by the Sales Manager.
* Maintain accurate and up\-to\-date records of leads, opportunities, and customer interactions in the CRM system.
* Collaborate closely with Marketing to convert qualified leads and provide field feedback to refine positioning and messaging.
* Support participation in medical conferences, trade shows, and industry events, representing UpSurgeOn's brand and products.
* Monitor market trends, competitor activity, and customer needs, feeding insights back to the commercial and product teams.
* Prepare quotations, commercial proposals, and support contract negotiation in coordination with management.

**Requisiti:**
--------------

**Essential**

* Proven experience (2\+ years) in a B2B sales role, ideally within medical devices, MedTech, healthcare, or a related technical/scientific field.
* Strong track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets.
* Excellent communication, negotiation, and relationship\-building skills.
* Fluency in English (spoken and written); Italian a strong plus. Additional languages are an advantage.
* Comfortable working with CRM tools (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot) and standard MS Office / Google Workspace applications.
* Willingness to travel domestically and internationally for client visits, demonstrations, and conferences.
* Self\-motivated, results\-oriented, and able to work autonomously as well as within a cross\-functional team.

**Preferred**

* Degree in Business, Life Sciences, Biomedical Engineering, or a related discipline.
* Existing network of contacts within hospitals, universities, or the surgical/medical training community.
* Prior experience selling capital equipment, training/education solutions, or simulation technologies.
* Familiarity with AR/VR technology or an interest in emerging MedTech innovation.

**Altre informazioni:**
-----------------------

**What UpSurgeOn does**

* The opportunity to join a fast\-growing, purpose\-driven MedTech company shaping the future of surgical education.
* Exposure to a global network of hospitals, universities, and medical societies.
* A dynamic, collaborative, and international work environment.
* Competitive salary package aligned with experience, plus performance\-based incentives.
* Opportunities for professional growth within a scaling commercial organization.

title: Project Manager for Diagnostic Imaging & Advanced Therapy - Campania, Italy (m/f/d)
description:
Join us in pioneering breakthroughs in healthcare. For everyone. Everywhere. Sustainably.


Our inspiring and caring environment forms a global community that celebrates diversity and individuality. We encourage you to step beyond your comfort zone, offering resources and flexibility to foster your professional and personal growth, all while valuing your unique contributions.


Apply now for the position of **Project Manager** for **Diagnostic Imaging e Advanced Therapy** business within Siemens Healthineers Project Management Function for the **Campania region, Italy**.**Your role:**

* You will plan and coordinate Siemens Healthineers projects according to the Project Management standards for the territory assigned (Campania region);
* You will analyze customer needs during the request for proposal phase, i.e visiting the customer site for room preparation to support internal and external stakeholders (suppliers, business partners as civil works and plan installation companies) for the necessary requirements related to customer quotations;
* You will analyze the technical specifications released by our factory and the national/international regulations to support the correct preparation of customer quotations;
* You will lead and manage the execution phase for the projects assigned, ensuring the fulfillment of contract specifications, requirements, clauses, costs and timing in collaboration with all the internal (Country/Head Quarter) and external stakeholders;
* You will be responsible for meeting all project milestones and technical objectives;
* You will monitor and report progress against milestones regularly to all stakeholders involved;
* You will comply with all current regulations, with specific reference to Health and Safety regulations and the Italian Public Procurement Code (Codice dei contratti pubblici);

**Your expertise:**

* Education: academic background in Electronic/Biomedical Engineering
* Experience: 3 or more years’ experience in project management into the Diagnostic Imaging business.
* Technical knowledge of Diagnostic Imaging equipment, including understanding of installation principles; construction/site (field) knowledge is considered an advantage.
* Project management knowledge;
* Technical knowledge of Diagnostic Imaging equipment;
* Excellent teamwork and territory management skills;
* Availability to travel within the country outside the assigned region/territory.
* Good English and Italian language skills.


To find out more about the specific business, have a look at ***www.siemens\-healthineers.com.***

***Compensation \& Benefits***


In accordance with our commitment to pay transparency and equal opportunity, we provide a clear breakdown of the financial package for this role:

* The position is regulated by the Chemical\-Pharmaceutical National Collective Labour Agreement (CCNL Chimico\-Farmaceutico) qualifica Impiegato.
* The base salary for this position typically ranges from €46\.500 to €62\.000 gross per year (RAL). The final offer will be tailored based on your individual skills, experience and specific location.
* Performance Bonus: this role is eligible for a variable incentive plan linked to specific achievements (10%)
* Total Rewards: beyond the base pay, we offer a comprehensive benefits package including a company car (also for private use), flexible working, health insurance and pension scheme. We are deeply committed to your growth, providing continuous learning opportunities with a dedicated budget for both technical advancement and soft skills development.

**Who we are:**
  

We are a team of more than 71,000 highly dedicated Healthineers in more than 70 countries. As a leader in medical technology, we constantly push the boundaries to create better outcomes and experiences for patients, no matter where they live or what health issues they are facing. Our portfolio is crucial for clinical decision\-making and treatment pathways.

**How we work:**  

When you join Siemens Healthineers, you become one in a global team of scientists, clinicians, developers, researchers, professionals, and skilled specialists, who believe in each individual’s potential to contribute with diverse ideas. We are from different backgrounds, cultures, religions, political and/or sexual orientations, and work together, to fight the world’s most threatening diseases and enable access to care, united by one purpose: to pioneer breakthroughs in healthcare. For everyone. Everywhere. Sustainably. Check our Careers Site atJobs at Siemens Healthineers AG.


As an equal opportunity employer, we welcome applications from individuals with disabilities.


We care about your data privacy and take compliance with GDPR as well as other data protection legislation seriously. For this reason, we ask you not to send us your CV or resume by email. Please **create a profile** within our talent community and subscribe to personalized job alerts that will keep you posted about new opportunities.

**To all recruitment agencies:**


Siemens Healthineers' recruitment is internally managed, with external support permitted only when a qualified supplier has established a formal contract with us. Unsolicited candidate submissions and referrals, absent a current supplier contract, do not establish consent and are ineligible for fees. We delete and destroy unsolicited information, thus, would recommend you refrain from any such practices. Your adherence to our policies is appreciated.

title: Data Manager for Clinical Trials
description:
**Company Description**  

PSI is a leading Contract Research Organization (CRO) with 30 years of experience in the pharmaceutical industry. Originated in Switzerland, PSI is a privately owned, full\-service CRO with a global reach, supporting clinical trials across multiple countries and continents. Our reputation for being highly selective about the projects we undertake highlights our commitment to delivering high\-quality, timely services across a broad spectrum of therapeutic indications.


At PSI, we foster an environment where a diverse range of colleagues feel welcomed and valued. Our inclusive culture is a cornerstone of our success, enabling us to attract and retain top talent globally.


We are not just about conducting clinical trials; we are about building a community where every team member has the opportunity to thrive and contribute to groundbreaking advancements in the pharmaceutical industry.

 **Job Description**  

Clincial Data Manager plays a critical role in the clinical research project. If you are detail\-oriented and committed to quality, able to work with large volume of clinical or medical data, you might be the professional we are looking for.

**Please submit your CV in English**

* Track database set\-up, testing, maintenance and data entry

* Proficiency in MS Office applications
* Organisational and time management skills
* Ability to work independently and multi\-task

  

**Additional Information** **Base salary range:** €34,000–€52,000 gross annual salary.  

**Actual compensation:** The salary offered within the stated range will depend on objective, job\-related criteria, including demonstrated competencies, qualifications, and skills relevant to the position.  

**Additional compensation:** bonus, incentive plans, and other benefits may apply.

title: Validation Manager
description:
**Position Summary**
--------------------



We are looking for a candidate that will join our team as a Validation Manger.


Management of the site EO TechTeam, ensuring that all EO validation activities are carried out in accordance with Customer requirements, STERIS policies \& procedures, relevant international standards, directives, and regulations.


Person in this role will be recognized as the primary subject matter expert \& leader in their region regarding EO sterilization technology including equipment and process qualification in accordance with regulatory requirements.


An integral member of the local management team, the role also supports other departments, including but not limited to; quality and regulatory inspections, root cause investigations, qualification and requalification of EO processing equipment and Continuous Improvement / Lean initiatives.

**Duties**
----------


**EO Technical Support \& Guidance** **(30%)**

* Directs and supports the regional business in matters such as harmonized policy and procedure development; best practice sharing; training \& development initiatives.
* Ensure harmonization across STERIS sites in EMEA APAC region by collaborating with other EO TechTeam leaders
* As the most senior technical person in the region, provide high\-level external support to Customers as required.
* Supports marketing team with Customer seminars and education activities e.g. Tech talks and Tech tips as required and appropriate.
* Ensure that all technical procedures and work instructions (local and global) are fully implemented and complied with.
* Support Customer qualification activities to ensure they are performed according to relevant standard requirements.
* Facilitates training of EO TechTeam members.
* Support Quality Team in document review (qualification, WI) and in technical assessment of critical suppliers.
* Regularly interfaces with Customers to ensure service levels and expectations are met in partnership with commercial / sales leadership – participates in business reviews (where required).
* Technical support and advice to other departments as required.

**Manages and coordinates the day\-to\-day activities of site EO TechTeam. (25%)**

* Leads EO TechTeam to perform according to the guidelines outlined in the STERIS Code of Business Conduct.
* Ensures the EO TechTeam is resourced with technically competent and trained personnel. Implement training plans in accordance with competency framework aligned to Job Descriptions.
* Drives a culture of positive employee relations, including responsiveness to safety, employee relations issues, disciplinary actions, communicating clear expectations and accountability processes, demonstrating effective leadership of the site EO TechTeam.
* Supports The STERIS AST business imperatives of Safety, Quality, Innovation, Sustainability and Lean and Customer Focus.
* Acts with integrity, treating everyone with mutual respect, exhibiting a genuine commitment to the success of all employees, and delivering satisfaction to our Customers through teamwork, innovation and continuous improvement.
* Recruitment, training and performance management reviews of direct reports within the EO TechTeam.
* Develops direct reports and ensures tools are in place to foster succession planning.
* Responsible for employee performance management of direct reports
* Allocation of Customer validation and/or equipment qualification activities to the site EO TechTeam.Ensures Qualifications are carried out in accordance with STERIS procedures and in compliance to relevant international standards, directives and regulations.

**Operational Qualification and Annual Requalification of EO Processing Equipment (20%)**

* Ensure EO processing equipment is appropriately qualified / re\-qualified in compliance with STERIS procedures, EN1422 and ISO 11135\.
* Where required, ensuring the operation of the data logger system (e.g. Pyrobutton, MadgeTech, etc.) for the site is maintained so that all loggers used in validation activity are within calibration.
**Duties (continued)**
----------------------


**EO Regulatory compliance (15%)**

* As an EO subject matter expert represent STERIS as required at relevant CEN and ISO standards and/or national mirror bodies.
* Support STERIS regulatory with implementation of regulatory facing activities such as FDA Master File
* As an EO SME support other STERIS departments including QA with EO compliance as required e.g. technical review of NCs (RCA, Corrective Action) \& Change control.

**Technology development and Innovation (10%)**

* Facilitates and promotes the advancement of the STERIS SEO program through activities such as Cycle Calculation and/or Parametric Release.
* Educates and promotes technical solutions offering to Customers
* Facilitate \& support EO focused R\&D initiatives as required e.g. cycle design, aeration optimization.
**Education Degree**
--------------------


* Bachelor's Degree in Engineering General or Science
**Minimum Work Experiance**
---------------------------


* A minimum of 5 years’ industrial experience in sterilization with comprehensive knowledge of relevant standards both technical and quality.
* Diploma/Degree in life\-science subject or Engineering along with relevant work\-related experience in industrial sterilization: EO, steam, irradiation, VHP.
**Specific Work Requirements**
------------------------------


* Ability to effectively read, write and verbally communicate in English.
* Ability to work under general direction guidance and leadership of a Supervisor or Director.
* Ability to work well with others.
* Above average organizing, analysing and math skills (advanced algebra and/or calculus and/or statistics) to determine organizational, Customer and regulatory problems and formulate corrective action plans; attention to detail.
* Knowledge of EO sterilization and validation in accordance with ISO11135
* Strong technical writing, scientific writing and problem\-solving skills
* Sound understanding of research methodologies
* Ability to complete statistical and data analysis
* Working knowledge of other relevant ISO standards and guidance documents (ISO10993\-7, ISO 11737\-1, ISO11138 series, ISO11137 series, EN 1422\)
* Proficient in use of MS Office and statistical tools
**Specific Work Preferences**
-----------------------------


* Ability to demonstrate mathematical and technical competence for problem solving.
* Excellent organizing and analysing skills with ability to determine organizational, Customer and regulatory problems and formulate corrective action plans.
* Proficient in use of MS Excel, MS Word, MS PowerPoint. Able to complete statistical and data analysis using statistical tools such as MS Excel.
* Ability to adapt to changing duties and responsibilities.
* Academic knowledge of Quality Systems and working in a regulated environment.
* Academic knowledge of Good Documentation Practices (GDP)
* Normal hearing range sufficient to hear alarms, bells, horns, etc.
**Specific Skills**
-------------------

* **EO Process \& Technical Knowledge \-** Demonstrates solid proficiency and understanding of ISO11135 and other ISO standards relating to the EO sterilization of medical devices
* **Initiative/Leadership** \- Self\-starter takes charge with little direction, seeks clarification if unsure of direction Proactively voices ideas, opinions, and recommendations, in a constructive way
* **Customer Focus \-** Demonstrate a “Customer first, always” mind set and a passion for serving each Customer by listening and identifying their needs.
* **Teamwork \-** Builds constructive and effective relationships and effectively manages through conflict while maintaining relationships.
* **Communication \-** Clear, concise communicator who can clearly express opinions and ideas while maintaining trust and credibility.
* **Job Knowledge/Business Acumen** \- Demonstrates solid proficiency in job function and understands how his/her position impacts the organization

----------------



In line with our commitment to transparency and equal pay, we provide the following information:

title: Client Services Specialist
description:
**Join us on our exciting journey!**


IQVIA™ is The Human Data Science Company™, focused on using data and science to help healthcare clients find better solutions for their patients. Formed through the merger of IMS Health and Quintiles, IQVIA offers a broad range of solutions that harness advances in healthcare information, Technology, analytics and human ingenuity to drive healthcare forward.


**Job Overview**
  

Under broad guidance, ensures clients obtain maximum value from products and services in order to facilitate a positive and productive long\-term relationship, either broad client or offering focused.


Essential Functions
  

* Ensures fulfillment of contract specifications and ad\-hoc client requests to build client satisfaction, under supervision.
* Assists team members to build, establish and maintain effective business relationships with clients to proactively support their needs as their day\-to\-day contact, possibly focused on a specific product or service.
* Ensures, through review and verification, that the product deliverable meets the customer's specifications, including timeliness and quality.
* Partners with the account manager and clients to derive optimum value from IQVIA products and services by explaining and expanding the usage of current IQVIA deliverables and identifying revenue opportunities within existing clients.
* Maintains effective internal communications with sales and client service support to keep all apprised on activities with the client.
* Monitors performance against existing service agreements and ensures client’s obligations are met.
* Researches, analyzes and responds to client that require an in\-depth understanding of the client, specific business line, and/or therapeutic market.
* May conduct product/services and process training for clients and other employees.
* May conduct service meetings at client sites.
* Represents IQVIA on external client projects.
* May mentor and lead other client service reps and represent the manager in their absence.

**Qualifications \- Our ideal candidate will have**
  

* Bachelor's Degree Req or Four (4\) years of related work experience Req
* One (1\) to two (2\) years experience as an IQVIA Client Service Representative Req
* Broad knowledge of the concepts, practices and procedures of the client service representative field Req
* Extensive knowledge of IQVIA products and the Pharmaceutical industry Req
* Meeting facilitation, presentation and training experience Req
* Proficiency in PC applications, such as Excel
* Ability to communicate effectively with various levels in the organization (written and oral) and to manipulate large transactional databases
* Demonstrated problem solving, analytical and strong customer service skills
* Demonstrated ability to develop and maintain relationships in a diverse business environment
* Ability to travel to clients and IQVIA offices as appropriate
* Accreditation preferred in multiple business lines of IMS service certification program Req

  

**\*\*\*PLEASE SUBMIT YOUR CV IN ENGLISH! THIS ROLE IS NOT OPEN TO VISA SPONSORSHIP.\*\*\***
We know that meaningful results require not only the right approach but also **the right people**. Regardless of your role, we invite you to reimagine healthcare with us. You will have the opportunity to play an important part in helping our clients drive healthcare forward and


**Whatever your career goals, we are here to ensure you get there!**


**We invite you to join IQVIA™.**


IQVIA is a leading global provider of clinical research services, commercial insights and healthcare intelligence to the life sciences and healthcare industries. We create intelligent connections to accelerate the development and commercialization of innovative medical treatments to help improve patient outcomes and population health worldwide. Learn more at https://jobs.iqvia.com


IQVIA is committed to integrity in our hiring process and maintains a zero tolerance policy for candidate fraud. All information and credentials submitted in your application must be truthful and complete. Any false statements, misrepresentations, or material omissions during the recruitment process will result in immediate disqualification of your application, or termination of employment if discovered later, in accordance with applicable law. We appreciate your honesty and professionalism.


The potential base pay range for this role, when annualized, is €38,000\.00 \- €57,000\.00\. The actual base pay offered may vary based on a number of factors including job\-related qualifications such as knowledge, skills, education, and experience; location; and/or schedule (full or part\-time). Dependent on the position offered, incentive plans, bonuses, and/or other forms of compensation may be offered, in addition to a range of health and welfare and/or other benefits.

title: Operations Manager
description:
**Title:** Operations Manager


**Location:** Gemonio, Italy
  

**Employment:** Permanent


**Come build something that matters.**

It takes great people to achieve greatness. People with a sense of purpose and integrity. People with a relentless pursuit of excellence. People who care about making things better For Those Who Make The World™. Sound like you? Join our top\-notch team of approximately 48,500 diverse and high\-performing professionals globally who are making their mark on some of the world’s most beloved brands, including DEWALT®, BLACK\+DECKER®, CRAFTSMAN®, STANLEY®, CUB CADET®, and HUSTLER®.


**The Job:**

As the **Operations Manager** , you’ll be part of our **Operations** team. You’ll get to:


* Coordinate and supervise all technical functions within the plant, including production, planning, engineering, maintenance, and continuous improvement, to achieve operational objectives.
* Translate the Plant Manager’s strategic directives into clear and actionable operational plans, ensuring effective execution across the site.
* Lead and develop technical teams by fostering collaboration, engagement, accountability, and professional growth.
* Monitor key operational performance indicators across functions and implement corrective actions to improve production, quality, efficiency, and cost performance.
* Promote a culture of continuous improvement by driving process optimization and innovation projects in collaboration with cross\-functional partners.
* Ensure effective planning of activities, resources, and priorities to meet timelines, budget expectations, and quality standards.
* Serve as the primary technical and managerial point of reference for operational functions within the plant.
* Champion a strong safety culture by promoting awareness, training, and adherence to company procedures and best practices.
* Monitor compliance with safety regulations and procedures, responding promptly to non\-conformities, risks, and emergency situations.
* Partner closely with HR, the Plant Manager, and the EHS Manager on workforce planning, talent development, performance management, and all activities related to employee health and safety at the site.

**The Person:**

You love to learn and grow and be acknowledged for your valuable contributions. You’re not intimidated by innovation. In fact, you embrace it. Wouldn’t it be great if you could do your job and do a world of good? You also have:


Proven experience in plant operations, manufacturing, or industrial management with responsibility across multiple technical functions. Strong leadership skills with the ability to manage, motivate, and develop teams in a fast\-paced production environment. Demonstrated ability to drive operational performance through KPI management, problem\-solving, and continuous improvement initiatives. Solid knowledge of workplace safety regulations, quality standards, and operational best practices within a manufacturing setting. Strong communication and collaboration skills, with the ability to work effectively across operations, HR, EHS, and site leadership.


**The Details:**

You’ll receive a competitive salary and a great benefits plan, including:


Discounts on Stanley Black \& Decker tools and other partner programs.


**And More:**

We want our company to be a place you’ll want to be – and stay. Being part of our team means you’ll get to:


**Grow:** Be part of our global company with 20\+ brands to grow and develop your skills along multiple career paths.


**Learn:** Have access to a wealth of learning resources, including our digital learning portal.


**Belong:** Experience an awesome place to work, where we have mutual respect and a great appreciation for a wide range of perspectives and experiences.


**Give Back:** Help us continue to make positive changes locally and globally through volunteerism, giving back, and sustainable business practices.


What’s more, you’ll get that pride that comes from empowering makers, doers, protectors, and everyday heroes all over the world. We’re more than the \#1 tools company. More than a driving force in outdoor power equipment. More than a global leader in industrial. We’re visionaries and innovators. As successful as we’ve been in the past, we have so much further to go. That’s where you come in. Join us!


*Salary Range: €50,000\.00 \- €80,000\.00*

*This is the lowest to highest salary range we would pay at the time of this posting. The salary offered in the range may vary depending on multiple factors including job level, geographic location, job related knowledge, skills, qualifications and experience.*

*The salary range listed in this job posting reflects the range for the primary location specified. The salary range may vary for other locations.*

*Please note that salary is only one component of total rewards at Stanley Black \& Decker.*

*The successful candidate may be eligible for annual merit increases.*

*The salary range and associated pay elements are established in line with Stanley Black \& Decker’s compensation structure. If collective bargaining agreements, sectoral agreements, works council arrangements, or statutory pay frameworks apply, they determine the salary range and additional pay elements.*

***We Don’t Just Build The World, We Build Innovative Technology Too.***

Joining the Stanley Black \& Decker team means working in an innovative, tech\-driven and highly collaborative team environment supported by over 43,500 professionals in 60 countries across the globe. Here, you’ll get the unique chance to impact some of the world’s most iconic brands including STANLEY TOOLS, DEWALT, CRAFTSMAN, MAC TOOLS and Black \+ Decker. Your ideas and solutions have the potential to reach millions of customers as we work together to write the next chapter in our history. Come build with us and take your career to new heights.


**Who We Are**

We’re the World’s largest tool company. We’re industry visionaries. We’re solving problems and advancing the manufacturing trade through innovative technology and our Industry 4\.0 Initiative. We are committed to ensuring our state\-of\-the\-art “smart factory” products and services provide greater quality to our customers \& greater environmental and social value to our planet. We are unique in that we have a rich and storied history dating back to 1843, but that hasn't stopped us from evolving into a vibrant, diverse, global growth company.


**Benefits \& Perks**

You’ll get a competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits plan that includes medical, dental, life, vision, wellness program, disability, retirement benefits, Employee Stock Purchase Plan, Paid Time Off, including paid vacation, holidays \& personal days, and tuition reimbursement. And, of course, discounts on Stanley Black \& Decker tools and products and well as discount programs for many other vendors and partners.


**What You’ll Also Get**

Career Opportunity: Career paths aren’t linear here. Being part of our global company with 60\+ brands gives you the chance to grow and develop your skills along multiple career paths.


**Learning \& Development:**  

Our lifelong learning philosophy means you’ll have access to a wealth of state\-of\-the\-art learning resources, including our Lean Academy and online university (where you can get certificates and specializations from renowned colleges and universities).


**Diverse \& Inclusive Culture:**  

We pride ourselves on being an awesome place to work. We respect and embrace differences because that’s how the best work gets done. You’ll find we like to have fun here, too.


**Purpose\-Driven Company:**  

You’ll help us continue to make positive changes in the local communities where we work and live as well as in the broader world through volunteerism, giving back and sustainable business practices.


**EEO Statement:**  

All qualified applicants to Stanley Black \& Decker are considered for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran’s status or any other protected characteristic.


If you require reasonable accommodation to complete an application or access our website, please contact us at (860\) 827\-3923 or at accommodations@sbdinc.com . Due to volume, we cannot respond to unrelated inquiries about the status of a completed application or resetting an account password.


Know Your Rights: Workplace discrimination is illegal (eeoc.gov)
