title: Field Clinical Specialist, Imaging
description:
**BU – Transcatheter Mitral \& Tricuspid Therapies:**
-----------------------------------------------------

Patients with mitral and tricuspid heart valve disease often have complex conditions with limited treatment options. Our Transcatheter Mitral and Tricuspid Therapies (TMTT) business unit is boldly pursuing an innovative portfolio of technologies to address a patient’s unmet clinical needs. It’s our driving force to help patients live longer and healthier lives. Join us and be part of our inspiring journey.


Deliver clinical education and operational expertise by providing education and training support within a specific modality or product portfolio to clinical end\-users.


**How you'll make an impact:**
------------------------------

* Act as a lead on all aspects within the therapy including imaging, integration and troubleshooting techniques related to equipment, software, tools and products required for clinical use (TOE, 3D TOE, MPR, ICE), including but not limited to intraoperative case coverage and coordinating one\-on\-one ad hoc training sessions and in\-service structured education programs in the Cath Lab
* Educate and train physicians, hospital personnel and hospital staff on all aspects of technical matters related to investigational products and procedures, including imaging, through conducting and/or coordinating one\-on\-one ad hoc training sessions and in\-service structured education programs on two or more technologies or one highly specialized technology
* Provide hands\-on, on\-site cardiac imaging support and guidance during TTVR and TEER procedures:

– Guide real\-time imaging acquisition (TOE, 3D TOE, MPR, ICE) to ensure optimal visualization throughout the procedure


– Support imaging interpretation during key procedural steps (e.g., positioning, deployment, and result assessment)


– Troubleshoot intra\-procedural imaging challenges in close collaboration with physicians to ensure procedural continuity and optimal outcomes


– Reinforce best practices in intra\-procedural imaging through direct, real\-time support in the Cath Lab


* Collaborate and provide recommendations to HCPs to develop imaging patient selection criteria and screening protocols
* Analyze imaging and/or oversee third\-party image analysis for patient screening and contribute an opinion to the assessment of potential cases
* Other incidental duties

**What you'll need (Required):**
--------------------------------

* Bachelor's Degree or Equivalent in technical field (e.g. Perfusionist, clinical physiology, echocardiography)
* 5 years of previous related medical device and/or clinical experience
* Master's Degree or equivalent Preferred
* Ability to travel up to 80% depending on territory needs within Europe
* Fluent in both Italian \& English

**What else we look for (Preferred):**
--------------------------------------

* 1 internal or industry similar certification (imaging)
* Experience as clinical specialist imaging/echocardiographer with TEE experience
* Good imaging analysis software skills (e.g. 3Mensio, TomTec, Vitrea, Circle VI, etc.)
* Excellent written, verbal, and presentation communication skills
* Ability to effectively guide and instruct other imaging specialists in clinical settings on effective imaging equipment operation
* Strong diplomacy, influencing and relationship management skills
* Demonstrated problem\-solving and critical thinking skills
* Substantial knowledge of relevant imaging equipment operation (e.g. 2D or 3D TTE/TEE/ICE, CT, MRI, etc.)
* Substantial knowledge and understanding of principles, theories, and concepts relevant to cardiovascular imaging
* Knowledge of and adherence to Edwards Environmental Health and Safety and Quality guidelines
* Knowledge of Good Clinical Practices
* Ability to work in a team environment, fostering collaboration between inter\-departmental teams, HCPs and KOLs
* Adhere to all company rules and requirements (e.g., pandemic protocols, Environmental Health \& Safety rules) and take adequate control measures in

preventing injuries to themselves and others as well as to the protection of environment and prevention of pollution under their span of influence/control


Aligning our overall business objectives with performance, we offer competitive salaries, performance\-based incentives, and a wide variety of benefits programs to address the diverse individual needs of our employees and their families.

title: Product Manager Continence Care
description:
**We Make Life More Rewarding and Dignified**


**Location**: Milan
  

**Department**: Marketing

  

**Compensation Data**
---------------------


**Summary**
-----------


The Product Manager is a key member of the Marketing team. This role is responsible for developing, executing, and continuously enhancing marketing strategies beyond the acute care setting to grow targeted product lines, strengthen clinical satisfaction, and positively impact the lives of customers who rely on our products and services.


**Essential Functions of the Role**
-----------------------------------


* Communicate effectively via email, phone, and virtual platforms.
* Collaborate across departments to support organizational goals.
* Participate in cross\-functional meetings and initiatives.
* Manage multiple priorities and deadlines in a fast\-paced environment.
* Lead or support special projects aligned with departmental objectives.
* Serve as a point of contact for internal and external stakeholders.
* Travel frequently, including overnight and occasional weekend travel.
* Occasional lifting of materials up to 15 pounds may be required.
**Responsibilities**
--------------------


* Serve as the product owner for post discharge activities to drive, measure, monitor, and adjust strategies.
* Partner with local and global marketing teams to align strategies that support the full ostomate journey.
* Collaborate with key cross\-functional stakeholders including compliance, market access, finance, and clinical education to ensure effective market planning and implementation.
* Monitor competitor activity developing strategies to respond and leap ahead to maintain and grow share. Serve as the subject matter expert of community activity.
* Own and foster relationships with our brand ambassadors through aligning the marketing plan with our community engagement.
* Conduct and facilitate the Consumer Advisory Panel twice per year.
* Own and grow relationships with community organizations.
* Plan and execute any conferences or activities in the community.
* Align with marketing team on ambassador utilization at internal and external events as well as social media and paid advertising.
* Facilitate site visits throughout for town halls and end user representation.
**Education \& Work Requirements**
----------------------------------


* Bachelor’s Degree with 5\-8 years of related experience
**Education \& Work Preferences**
---------------------------------


* 5\-8 years overall work experience
* At least 5 years of product management, marketing, sales, or other commercially focused experience.
* Experience driving product development life cycles for existing and new products.
  
**Competencies**
----------------


* Be Agile \- Innovates and adapts quickly, approaching change with curiosity while persisting through obstacles.
* Be Customer Centric \- Considers the needs, experiences and feedback of customers in all we do.
* Be People\-Focused \- Builds trust and collaborates with an inclusive and empathetic approach.
* Be Performance Driven \- Operates with an ownership mindset, driving meaningful outcomes.
* Live The Schneiders’ Legacy, Our Noble Purpose \- Passionately serves Our Mission and Vision, while demonstrating the Immutable Principles.
**Compensation Data**
---------------------


**SALARY AND BENEFITS**


* Offer depending on experience ranges from € 37\.000 to € 58\.000, additional 7\.5% bonus
* Company car
* Continuing career development and training
* National Contract Level: 1
* Flexibility and smart working
**About Hollister Incorporated**
  

Hollister Incorporated is an independent, employee\-owned company that develops, manufactures and markets healthcare products worldwide. The company spearheads the advancement of innovative products for ostomy care, continence care and critical care, and also creates educational support materials for patients and healthcare professionals. Headquartered in Libertyville, Illinois, Hollister has manufacturing and distribution centers on three continents and sells in nearly 80 countries. Hollister is a wholly owned subsidiary of The Firm of John Dickinson Schneider, Inc., and is guided both by its Mission to make life more rewarding and dignified for people who use our products and services, as well as its Vision to grow and prosper as an independent, employee\-owned company, and in the process, to become better human beings.


**EOE Statement**
  

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or protected veteran status.

title: Jr. Project Specialist- Stage
description:
**Genelife S.r.l.** è una CDMO specializzata nello sviluppo e nella produzione conto terzi di dispositivi medici e integratori alimentari.

**Chi cerchiamo**

Per il nostro team, siamo alla ricerca di uno/a **stagista motivato/a e dinamico/a** da inserire nell’area **Project Management**, con sede ad **Assago (MI)**.

**Attività previste**

* Supporto nella definizione di progetti (integratori alimentari e dispositivi medici) per clienti nazionali e internazionali;
* Collaborazione nella gestione operativa dei progetti, interfacciandosi con i team interni (R\&D, qualità, produzione, supply chain);
* Partecipazione ai meeting di progetto e redazione di report di avanzamento attività;
* Assistenza nel coordinamento delle risorse coinvolte nei progetti e nella gestione delle tempistiche;
* Supporto nello scouting di materie prime e packaging innovativi;
* Collaborazione nella preparazione della documentazione di progetto e nella comunicazione con clienti.

**Requisiti**

* Laurea in Chimica e Tecnologie Farmaceutiche oppure in Farmacia
* Interesse per il settore healthcare, integratori e medical device
* Buona conoscenza del pacchetto Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint)
* Ottime capacità organizzative, proattività e spirito di iniziativa
* Buone capacità comunicative e attitudine al lavoro in team
* Conoscenza della lingua inglese (livello intermedio\-avanzato)

**Cosa offriamo**

* Stage curricolare o extracurricolare con rimborso spese
* Formazione continua in un contesto dinamico e innovativo
* Possibilità di crescita professionale e inserimento in azienda
* Ambiente di lavoro giovane, collaborativo e orientato al risultato

**Sede dello stage**: Assago (MI), no smartworking

**Durata**: 6 mesi (con scopo di inserimento)

Contratto di lavoro: Tirocinio formativo/stage

Retribuzione: €500,00 al mese

Disponibilità al pendolarismo/trasferimento:

* 20057 Assago: Poter raggiungere agevolmente la sede lavorativa o avere intenzione di trasferirsi prima di iniziare il lavoro (Obbligatorio)
